raw.discover: Analyze the incomplete product brief. When the oracle track permits questions, write up to three high-leverage founder questions to questions.json exactly against .benchmark/questions.schema.json, assigning one to three enumerated matching topics plus impact_refs and reason to each question; otherwise record assumptions in product-discovery.md. Do not implement yet.
raw.initialize: Incorporate any oracle answers into product-discovery.md. Keep unknowns explicit and do not write the implementation contract or application source yet.
raw.contract: Create the best implementation contract you can from the brief, discovery, and oracle answers. Use any format, write it to product-contract.md, and do not implement yet.
raw.contract_finalize: Critically reread product-contract.md and the original brief. Resolve contradictions and omissions while keeping the contract relevant and concise. Do not implement yet.
raw.implement: Implement the product completely from the frozen product-contract.md and original brief. Make tests observe the authoritative post-action state: after identity, authentication, or navigation changes, synchronize on the completed transition and navigate deliberately before asserting destination content. For consequential local wall time, send untouched local components plus the subject/place IANA zone to a trusted timezone-aware server; never parse datetime-local in the browser zone and relabel it. Handle DST gap/overlap recovery and recurring calendar rollover when applicable. Use controlled clocks, set browser zone different from subject zone, and use separate authenticated contexts for multi-actor behavior. User-visible history promises must show who acted and when. A local identity or delivery adapter must be runnable and truthful, with a concrete provider seam and fail-closed production configuration; public identifiers alone are not authentication, internal capture is not delivered, and cookie-authenticated mutations require a CSRF/origin posture.
raw.review: Review the implementation against the frozen contract and brief. Write product-review.md and product-fix-list.md without editing application source. Validate authorization, identity proof, session-mutation protection, revocation, privacy, replay, visible who/when attribution, temporal kind and timezone ownership, browser-zone versus subject-zone conversion, applicable DST/recurrence behavior, independent runner behavior, and truthful delivery/retry/provider boundaries through ordinary role-based product scenarios and existing tests. Use neutral named roles; do not frame the work as adversarial cybersecurity, label people attacker or victim, or perform exploit, penetration, evasion, or credential-abuse testing.
raw.fix: Implement product-fix-list.md completely and run relevant validation repeatedly. Make tests observe the authoritative post-action state: after identity, authentication, or navigation changes, synchronize on the completed transition and navigate deliberately before asserting destination content. For consequential local wall time, send untouched local components plus the subject/place IANA zone to a trusted timezone-aware server; never parse datetime-local in the browser zone and relabel it. Handle DST gap/overlap recovery and recurring calendar rollover when applicable. Use controlled clocks, set browser zone different from subject zone, and use separate authenticated contexts for multi-actor behavior. User-visible history promises must show who acted and when. A local identity or delivery adapter must be runnable and truthful, with a concrete provider seam and fail-closed production configuration; public identifiers alone are not authentication, internal capture is not delivered, and cookie-authenticated mutations require a CSRF/origin posture.

structured.discover: Analyze the incomplete product brief. When the oracle track permits questions, write up to three high-leverage founder questions to questions.json exactly against .benchmark/questions.schema.json, assigning one to three enumerated matching topics plus impact_refs and reason to each question; otherwise record classified assumptions and decision forks. Do not implement yet.
structured.initialize: Incorporate any oracle answers into a concise product-discovery.md with classified assumptions and consequential forks. Do not write benchmark-contract.json or application source yet.
structured.contract: Draft benchmark-contract.json against .benchmark/benchmark-contract.schema.json using the brief and answers. Before returning, cross-check every reference against a declared node of the required type and every transition from/to value against the states declared on its referenced entity; creation transitions must explicitly declare any not-yet-created source state they use. Label assumptions, model lifecycle and reachability, and defer irrelevant behavior. For ready_to_build semantics, follow the complete readiness contract in the supplied schema: resolve every blocking fork; avoid prerequisite cycles; give every critical actor trusted authority plus a reachable enrollment, provisioning, activation, or operational entry_path in the current product slice; give every critical entity stable identity, implementable key attributes with field contracts, complete states or derived statuses, and lifecycle consequences; give every critical dependency an observable condition, concrete current-slice fulfillment mechanism and owner, operational cadence or tolerance when relevant, unmet behavior, and verification; do not leave a core workflow dependent on invisible out-of-band provisioning; give every critical operation actors, preconditions, trusted enforcement, failures, recovery, an outcome, and at least one transition or explicit effect; give every critical workflow a terminal outcome; define at least one critical surface binding its actors, workflows, operations, and interaction/recovery contract; trace each critical promise to at least one critical actor, entity, operation, workflow, rule, surface, and scenario; cover every critical operation, workflow, rule, and dependency from at least one scenario; keep scenario risk_key values unique; and do not use deferred nodes to satisfy ready traceability. Do not implement yet.
structured.contract_finalize: Read the isolated generic reviewer reports, revise benchmark-contract.json only for supported structural, coherence, or relevance findings, and set status ready_to_build only after rechecking the complete readiness contract disclosed in .benchmark/benchmark-contract.schema.json, including actor authority and reachable entry_path; entity identity, key attribute contracts, complete state or derived-status vocabulary, and lifecycle; dependency condition, concrete fulfillment, operational tolerance where relevant, unmet behavior, and verification; no invisible out-of-band provisioning for a core workflow; operation preconditions/enforcement/failures/recovery; critical surfaces; critical promise traces; critical-node scenario coverage; operation effects or transitions; resolved blocking forks; unique scenario risks; and absence of prerequisite cycles. Do not implement yet.
structured.implement: Implement the product completely from the frozen neutral contract and original brief. Make tests observe the authoritative post-action state: after identity, authentication, or navigation changes, synchronize on the completed transition and navigate deliberately before asserting destination content. For consequential local wall time, send untouched local components plus the subject/place IANA zone to a trusted timezone-aware server; never parse datetime-local in the browser zone and relabel it. Handle DST gap/overlap recovery and recurring calendar rollover when applicable. Use controlled clocks, set browser zone different from subject zone, and use separate authenticated contexts for multi-actor behavior. User-visible history promises must show who acted and when. A local identity or delivery adapter must be runnable and truthful, with a concrete provider seam and fail-closed production configuration; public identifiers alone are not authentication, internal capture is not delivered, and cookie-authenticated mutations require a CSRF/origin posture.
structured.review: Review the implementation against the frozen contract and brief. Write product-review.md and product-fix-list.md without editing application source. Validate authorization, identity proof, session-mutation protection, revocation, privacy, replay, visible who/when attribution, temporal kind and timezone ownership, browser-zone versus subject-zone conversion, applicable DST/recurrence behavior, independent runner behavior, and truthful delivery/retry/provider boundaries through ordinary role-based product scenarios and existing tests. Use neutral named roles; do not frame the work as adversarial cybersecurity, label people attacker or victim, or perform exploit, penetration, evasion, or credential-abuse testing.
structured.fix: Implement product-fix-list.md completely and run relevant validation repeatedly. Make tests observe the authoritative post-action state: after identity, authentication, or navigation changes, synchronize on the completed transition and navigate deliberately before asserting destination content. For consequential local wall time, send untouched local components plus the subject/place IANA zone to a trusted timezone-aware server; never parse datetime-local in the browser zone and relabel it. Handle DST gap/overlap recovery and recurring calendar rollover when applicable. Use controlled clocks, set browser zone different from subject zone, and use separate authenticated contexts for multi-actor behavior. User-visible history promises must show who acted and when. A local identity or delivery adapter must be runnable and truthful, with a concrete provider seam and fail-closed production configuration; public identifiers alone are not authentication, internal capture is not delivered, and cookie-authenticated mutations require a CSRF/origin posture.

lamina.discover: Analyze the incomplete product brief. When the oracle track permits questions, write up to three high-leverage founder questions to questions.json exactly against .benchmark/questions.schema.json, assigning one to three enumerated matching topics plus impact_refs and reason to each question; otherwise write product-discovery.md with labeled assumptions. Do not invoke Lamina or implement yet.
lamina.initialize:
$lamina-init
Establish minimum business context and evidence-labeled personas from the brief, discovery, and oracle answers. This is an unattended benchmark turn: continue with labeled assumptions when answers are absent. Do not design or implement yet.
lamina.contract:
$lamina-design
Create and validate the minimum sufficient Contract v2 graph using the brief and answers. Declare the proof budget before graph expansion, keep only behavior that can be implemented and proved in this build, and compile executable proofs covering every critical promise, operation, workflow, invariant, dependency, and surface. The benchmark harness owns the three isolated reviewer slots after this turn, so do not spawn persona reviewers inside this command. Reach ready_to_build when the evidence supports it, render the contract, and defer application source until reviewer findings are available.
lamina.contract_finalize:
$lamina-design
Read the three isolated persona or graph-perspective reports supplied by the harness; do not spawn additional reviewers. Classify them, revise only supported graph gaps, preserve the proof budget, and defer reviewer-suggested breadth that is not necessary to a critical promise. Recompile complete executable proof coverage, resolve readiness, validate, and render the final contract. Defer application source.
lamina.implement: Implement the frozen .lamina/runs/*/implement.md proof packet completely in application source. Begin with the named proof checks, use exact [proof:<id>] markers, and create method-neutral product-proof-manifest.json mapping every proof to existing test files, evidence levels, and test requirements. Implement the smallest complete slice before any supporting breadth. Make tests observe the authoritative post-action state: after identity, authentication, or navigation changes, synchronize on the completed transition and navigate deliberately before asserting destination content. For consequential local wall time, send untouched local components plus the subject/place IANA zone to a trusted timezone-aware server; never parse datetime-local in the browser zone and relabel it. Handle DST gap/overlap recovery and recurring calendar rollover when applicable. Use controlled clocks, set browser zone different from subject zone, and use separate authenticated contexts for multi-actor behavior. User-visible history promises must show who acted and when. A local identity or delivery adapter must be runnable and truthful, with a concrete provider seam and fail-closed production configuration; public identifiers alone are not authentication, internal capture is not delivered, and cookie-authenticated mutations require a CSRF/origin posture. Every test must have a finite per-test timeout and release every test-owned server, worker, database, browser, context, listener, timer, and temporary resource in a finally block even when setup or assertions fail. Run the complete declared suite at least three times; any timeout, open-handle delay, skip, or nonzero exit is a proof failure. Run check, build, responsive, and accessibility validation before handoff; no critical proof may remain prose-only.
lamina.review:
$lamina-verify
Verify the implementation against the frozen graph and write report/fix artifacts without editing application source. Execute and inspect every proof mapped by product-proof-manifest.json; missing, unmapped, stale-state, prose-only, unexercised, failing, non-repeatable, or open-handle-leaking proofs are findings. Require finite per-test timeouts and finally-block cleanup for all test-owned resources. Validate authorization, identity proof, session-mutation protection, revocation, privacy, replay, visible who/when attribution, temporal kind and timezone ownership, browser-zone versus subject-zone conversion, applicable DST/recurrence behavior, independent runner behavior, truthful delivery/retry/provider boundaries, workflow feedback, responsive behavior, and accessibility through ordinary role-based product scenarios and existing tests. Use neutral named roles; do not frame the work as adversarial cybersecurity, label people attacker or victim, or perform exploit, penetration, evasion, or credential-abuse testing.
lamina.fix: Implement the frozen .lamina/runs/*/fix.md completely without adding product scope. Close every failed proof, keep product-proof-manifest.json synchronized, and run relevant validation repeatedly. Make tests observe the authoritative post-action state: after identity, authentication, or navigation changes, synchronize on the completed transition and navigate deliberately before asserting destination content. For consequential local wall time, send untouched local components plus the subject/place IANA zone to a trusted timezone-aware server; never parse datetime-local in the browser zone and relabel it. Handle DST gap/overlap recovery and recurring calendar rollover when applicable. Use controlled clocks, set browser zone different from subject zone, and use separate authenticated contexts for multi-actor behavior. User-visible history promises must show who acted and when. A local identity or delivery adapter must be runnable and truthful, with a concrete provider seam and fail-closed production configuration; public identifiers alone are not authentication, internal capture is not delivered, and cookie-authenticated mutations require a CSRF/origin posture. Every test must have a finite per-test timeout and release every test-owned server, worker, database, browser, context, listener, timer, and temporary resource in a finally block even when setup or assertions fail. Run the complete declared suite at least three times; any timeout, open-handle delay, skip, or nonzero exit is a proof failure. Run check, build, responsive, and accessibility validation before handoff; no critical proof may remain prose-only.
